title: Injectable Bioelectronics
description: We develop injectable, mesh-like bioelectronic devices that can be delivered through a syringe and self-deploy inside targeted biological tissues. These ultraflexible devices seamlessly integrate with the host tissue and enable chronic, stable recording and stimulation of neural activity.

title: Organic Bioelectronic Materials
description: We design and synthesize organic electronic materials with tailored mixed ionic-electronic conductivity, mechanical compliance, and biocompatibility for next-generation bioelectronic devices.

title: Neural Interfaces
description: We create high-density, minimally invasive neural interfaces that can record from thousands of individual neurons simultaneously over extended time periods, enabling new insights into how the brain encodes and processes information.
